calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc., T:415.746.1477 ext.217, for petitioner/moving party;
Steven Myers, Esq. of the Law Offices of John A. Biard, T:925.945.4492, for respondents/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER ANGELICA STRONG'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is DENIED. Travelers did not take any of the three actions prescribed by Insurance Code 12580.2(i)(1) within two years of the date of her accident which is required to maintain a claim against respondents for coverage for the accident. The November 7, 2016 email sent by Ms. Strong's attorney does not constitute a formal institution of arbitration proceedings per Insurance Code 12580.2(i)(1)(C). Insurance Code 12580.2(i)(3) provides that this court, not an arbitrator, decides whether the doctrine of equitable estoppel excuses Ms. Strong's noncompliance with section 12580.2(i)(1). Even considering the facts submitted with the reply papers, Ms. Strong failed to show that the doctrine of equitable estoppel excuses her noncompliance with section 12580.2(i)(1). (Kunstman v. Mirizzi (1965) 234 Cal. App. 2d 753). Kuntsman, which is consistent with more recent equitable estoppel case law, held there is no estoppel where an insurer affirmed its principled position that it was willing to settle a claim while asking counsel for the insured for a medical report to enable it to settle the claim. As here, in Kuntsman the insurer did not make any promise to toll the statute of limitation nor did it make any fraudulent representations. Kuntsman is on-point authority requiring the rejection of Ms. Strong's estoppel argument. The cases relied on by Ms. Strong are distinguishable because in all of them there was an affirmative request for delay made to the injured party, an affirmative promise to satisfy the claim, and/or a misrepresentation about the claim.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
